title: goeppertia wallisii
intro: Goeppertia wallisii, also known as Calathea Wallis, is a species of plant within the Marantaceae family, commonly referred to as a prayer plant due to its unique foliage that folds up at night like hands in prayer. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Goeppertia wallisii also extend to its potential in reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality, thanks to its adaptogenic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
